page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1ne.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor. It features 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, supported by a 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that this instance delivers entry-level performance. In CPU-focused evaluations, such as stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, the server scores in the bottom 25% tier. Memory performance is also constrained, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth falling into the bottom 10% tier and write bandwidth in the bottom 25%. Similarly, single-threaded and multi-threaded decompression tasks, along with static web serving throughput, rank in the bottom 10% of tested systems.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation but lower-tier benchmark performance, this instance represents a legacy compute-optimized option. It is best suited for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high-throughput memory or modern instruction sets like AVX-512 VNNI. Appropriate workloads include lightweight background processing, basic utility services, and development or testing environments where predictable CPU access is required without high performance demands.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1ne.xlarge is an x86_64 server instance featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor with 4 physical cores, 4 vCPUs, and a 2.5 GHz clock speed. Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, it provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, classifying it as a compute-optimized configuration. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this server in the lower tiers, with CPU and memory bandwidth results generally falling in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested systems. Consequently, this instance is best utilized for lightweight, non-intensive workloads such as basic utility services, development environments, and simple background tasks that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation without requiring high memory throughput.

tagline: A dedicated four-core compute-optimized instance with eight gigabytes of memory, designed for entry-level utility workloads and basic development environments.
